title: Tactical Mesh Network Transmission Layer
subtitle: Defence hackathon project
summary: A hackathon concept for UDP-style communication over raw 802.11 packets in degraded environments. The Rust radio library was provided externally; our work focused on the Python layer, protocol design, node behavior, and the simplest working transmission demo. The project won the transmission layer category.
imageAlt: Tactical mesh network transmission cover image

title: DPM - Dumb Packet Manager
subtitle: Infrastructure project course
summary: DPM is a limited-scope production level tool for reproducible lab and CTF environment setup. It installs curated tools and profiles through a Go CLI, Rust TUI, signed remote index, and project website. My work focused on project management, presentations, website work, adapter internals, tests, CLI work, sync-index, and search.

title: Linux Defaults with Ansible
subtitle: Server management course project
summary: A small Ansible project that turns a fresh Debian or Ubuntu host into a more usable baseline system with firewall rules, fail2ban, automatic updates, SSH hardening, and tmux defaults. My part focused on the default_security role and the security automation.
imageAlt: Linux defaults with Ansible cover image

title: Zero-Trust Home Network Architecture
subtitle: Homelab Project
summary: This homelab project demonstrates how I build a secure, segmented home network using affordable hardware and security architecture principles. It covers hardware selection, VLAN design, build process, and operational practices for strong isolation and minimal attack surface.

title: H-T8 Club Website
subtitle: Website
summary: The H-T8 website is created for a student hacking club focused on cybersecurity and ethical hacking. This article delves into the technical journey behind the website's creation, highlighting JavaScript-driven features and implementation details that bring functionality and interactivity to life.

title: Simple TCP-Server
subtitle: Small Python project
summary: This project is a simple TCP server that listens for incoming connections on a specified port. The server will respond with a message to the client when a connection is established. The server will then wait for the client to send a message, and when the client sends a message, the server will respond with a message to the client. The server will continue to wait for messages from the client until the client sends a message that says "exit". When the server receives a message that says "exit", the server will close the connection and terminate.

title: Portfolio Website V2.0.1
subtitle: Portfolio Website
summary: This is the second version of my personal portfolio website. The website is designed to show my original style and showcase my skills and projects. For now this is a static website, but I am planning to add more functionality in the future.

title: WebGoat running on Docker
subtitle: Containerizing Hacking Platform
summary: This was a small project that I did as part of my studies in cybersecurity. The goal was to containerize a hacking platform called WebGoat using Docker. The project was a great learning experience, as it allowed me to get hands-on experience with containerization and learn more about how Docker works.

title: TestoMonster Encryption
subtitle: Javascript encryption algorithm
summary: TestoMonster is a simple encryption algorithm that we created using Javascript. TestoMonster takes this idea and adds a few extra features to make the encryption more secure and difficult to crack.
